Medicare, simplified

The Four Things Most People Need to Know

Medicare can feel overwhelming. Here's a quick, friendly overview of the main pieces — explained without jargon.

Medicare Advantage

All-in-one plans with extra benefits like dental, vision, hearing, and prescription coverage.

Learn more

Medicare Supplement

Lower surprise medical costs with predictable monthly premiums and broad doctor flexibility.

Learn more

Prescription Drug Plans

Help lower medication expenses with the right Part D plan for your specific drugs.

Learn more

Turning 65

Learn enrollment timelines, avoid penalties, and start your Medicare journey on the right foot.

Learn more
Watch & learn

Medicare, Explained in Plain English

Short, friendly videos on the questions we hear most — with a transcript for every one. Pick a topic to watch.

Medicare 101 Watch the video

Understanding Medicare Parts A, B, C & D

A 2-minute tour of the four parts of Medicare and what each one covers.

Why families choose us

Why Utah Families Choose AltaMedicare

We believe Medicare guidance should feel calm, clear, and personal — never pushy. Here's what makes our approach different:

  • ✓ Personalized Medicare guidance based on your specific situation
  • ✓ Simple, jargon-free explanations without sales pressure
  • ✓ Help understanding and lowering prescription costs
  • ✓ Annual plan reviews to make sure you're still in the right plan
  • ✓ Local Utah support — we live and work where you do
  • ✓ One-on-one consultations at your pace
  • ✓ Educational approach — we want you to understand, not just sign

Bret Swope

Founder · Licensed Medicare Agent

"My goal is to make Medicare feel less confusing — so you can make confident decisions for yourself and your family. My consultations are always free."

Prescription Costs Too High?

From our clients

What Utah Families Are Saying

Common questions

Medicare Questions, Answered

Quick answers to the questions we hear most often. Don't see yours? Just give us a call.

Most people pay no premium for Part A (hospital). Part B (medical) has a standard monthly premium set each year by Medicare. Beyond that, your costs depend on which plan type you choose — Medicare Advantage plans often have very low or even $0 premiums, while Medicare Supplement plans have a separate monthly premium in exchange for lower out-of-pocket costs. We'll walk through real numbers based on your situation.

It depends on the plan type. Original Medicare and Medicare Supplement plans let you see any doctor in the U.S. who accepts Medicare. Medicare Advantage plans use networks, so you'll want to check that your preferred doctors are in-network before enrolling. We can verify this for you before you choose.

You become eligible for Medicare. Your Initial Enrollment Period is a 7-month window — the 3 months before your 65th birthday month, your birthday month, and the 3 months after. Missing this window can lead to lifetime late penalties, so it's important to plan ahead. Our Turning 65 page walks through the timeline in detail.

Medicare Advantage (Part C) bundles your hospital, medical, and usually drug coverage into one plan from a private insurer — often with extras like dental and vision, but with networks and copays. Medicare Supplement (Medigap) works alongside Original Medicare and covers most of what Medicare doesn't pay, giving you predictable costs and any-doctor flexibility. Different needs, different fits — we'll help you compare.

Yes — every fall there's an Annual Enrollment Period (October 15 through December 7) when you can switch plans for the following year. There are also Special Enrollment Periods if you move, lose other coverage, or qualify for low-income help. We do free annual reviews for our clients to make sure their plan still fits.

Most Medicare Advantage plans include prescription drug coverage. If you choose Original Medicare or a Supplement, you'll typically add a standalone Part D drug plan. Either way, the right choice depends on which specific medications you take — that's why we always review your drug list before recommending anything.

No matching questions — just ask Bret directly →

Schedule a Call

We do not offer every plan available in your area. Currently we represent organizations which offer plans in Utah. Please contact Medicare.gov, 1-800-MEDICARE, or your local State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP) to get information on all of your options.
